changes.before: And you were told last chapter's proof turns to nothing if we reach the living too late.
changes.after: And you were told Millhaven told us nothing we can use if the living die before I can read them.

changes.before: necromantic contamination, bodily spread, proof already north of here
changes.after: Jaina's answer crowded her teeth.

changes.before: Jaina could feel it happen the way she felt a ward settle
changes.after: The square changed around that.

changes.before: You can argue with a report after she's done.
changes.after: You can argue after she's done.

changes.before: understanding settle in his face faster than she had expected
changes.after: The line in his face shifted.

changes.before: It was so dry Jaina only barely caught the humor in it. The captain caught it too...
changes.after: Dry enough that Jaina nearly missed the humor. The captain caught it; the line in his face shifted.

changes.before: built for weddings, meetings, winter suppers, all the dull communal things villages did to prove they would still exist come spring
changes.after: built for weddings, meetings, winter suppers

changes.before: The wrongness was not sitting in them like poison waiting out its hour. It was working.
changes.after: Alive, it was busier. Greedier. It was working.

changes.before: A boy... as if the thing had knocked once... An old woman... well-fed and settled.
changes.after: A boy... carried it only faintly. An old woman... carried it deeper.

changes.before: The useful things were often the ones people forgot to count because they had not looked dramatic enough.
changes.after: He had not expected the question.

changes.before: the medic had probably been forcing food into people who needed strength and did not know he was feeding the thing already chewing through them
changes.after: A knife left in a loaf on the trestle.

changes.before: Millhaven had given her nothing to compare... Repeated feedings, not one bad swallow...
changes.after: Millhaven had given her nothing to split apart. Here the living had done that work for her.

changes.before: Not the Light.
changes.after: Grain.

changes.before: Jaina saw the dust on that glove first.
changes.after: Grain dust filmed the glove.

changes.before: It's a scene.
changes.after: It's evidence.

changes.before: Mixed? Washed? Stored separate? Something in liquid form would survive transport better than raw casting.
changes.after: Mixed, washed, kept apart. If it started in liquid form, it would travel better.

changes.before: without sounding surprised by your own success
changes.after: without sounding wounded by success

changes.before: —not on open grain. On stone...
changes.after: Frost sheeted across the boards under it, thin and sudden.

changes.before: Sweetness hit the room like a hand over the mouth.
changes.after: Sweetness hit the room hard and close.

changes.before: Recognition without a proper name moved through her.
changes.after: Recognition came without a proper name.

changes.before: Some sacks had been disturbed that morning. Others had a finer settled coat...
changes.after: Grain dust lay over everything, but not evenly.

changes.before: Not proof of source. Proof of route.
changes.after: Route, if not source.

changes.before: boys with frightened faces who had been told to mind one corner of the machine and believe it was too small to matter
changes.after: boys told to mind one corner of the machine and believe it small

changes.before: Bitter almond, crushed shell, a smell so brief and murderous...
changes.after: Crushed kernel. Poison. Quick and thorough.

changes.before: this time she let the full speed into her voice because he had earned the right to keep up
changes.after: Listen carefully, she said to the medic.

changes.before: You'll want the medic's triage order written down before we lose the shape of this one.
changes.after: You'll want the order of the sick written down before we lose the shape of this.

changes.before: Dry again. Flat enough to pass for simple sense. But useful. Load-bearing.
changes.after: Dry again. Flat enough to pass for simple sense. But useful.

flags: Canon check passed: Jaina as Antonidas's apprentice and plague investigator, living Sylvanas as Ranger-General, and Andorhal as a grain hub all align with Warcraft canon.
Kept 'Sweet Light' as in-world religious speech.
Final pass cleared modern procedural leaks: removed 'last chapter', 'scene', and 'triage order'.

flaggedForExpander.reason: Mother's reaction lands in one line; expansion pass could deepen the body-level fear if later pacing allows.
Captain-nephew fallout is intentionally brisk here; expansion pass could thicken the emotional recoil without changing the plot.
